message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We propose a novel
                  <jats:bold>Timed Intervention</jats:bold>
                  exended SEIR model for predicting the evolution of the Covid 19 Pandemic in the USA. The model can, by sparameter, assignment, be reduced to the model of Peng et al, which appeared in February, 2020 (Reference [2]). Novel aspects of the proposed model include
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Formulation of a “Protected” population
                        <jats:italic>P</jats:italic>
                        , which can be viewed as a “Sheltered in Place”, unexposed population which, starting at time
                        <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                        =
                        <jats:italic>
                          τ
                          <jats:sub>P</jats:sub>
                        </jats:italic>
                        , builds up and stores a reservoir of unexposed Population;
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        This “Protection Intervention” provides the basis for a second Timed Release Intervention: on receiving a “reopening signal” at time
                        <jats:italic>t</jats:italic>
                        =
                        <jats:italic>
                          τ
                          <jats:sub>R</jats:sub>
                        </jats:italic>
                        , this second intervention initiates a release of this stored population back into the general population
                        <jats:italic>S</jats:italic>
                        .;
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Selection of model parameters to optimize the approximation of the model up to the present, and then projecting simulation of the model based on the value thus obtained 100,200, 365, and 730 days into the future.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  This model shows excellent qualitative results and quantitative results that are good, considering the chosen nationwide scope. These results compare favorably to University of Washington IHME [5] projections, as published Daily on the
                  <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="http://Worldometers.info">http://Worldometers.info</jats:ext-link>
                  website. The qualitative and quantitative behavior of all 7 state variables
                  <jats:italic>S, P, E, I, Q, R, D</jats:italic>
                  will be illustrated and discussed, in this and future further Intervention cycles
                  <jats:sup>1</jats:sup>
                </jats:p>
title: The Futures of the Pandemic in the USA : A Timed Intervention Model

reference.unstructured: G. Hachtel et al. The sparse tableau to network analysis and design. IEEE TRANSACTIONS ON CIRCUIT THEORY V, CT-18(1), JANUARY 1971.
et al L. Hong . Epidemic analysis of covid-19 in china by dynamical modeling. arXiv, 2002.06563v1, February 2020.
E.N Lorenz . The nature and theory of the general circulation of the atmosphere. WMO Bulletin, April 1967.
et al Na Yi . Analysis and control of an seir epidemic system with nonlinear transmission rate. Mathematical and Computer Modeling, 2009.
DPhil Professor Christopher J. L. Murray, MD. 2020.
D. Fanelli F. Piazza T. Carletti . The unreasonable effectiveness of simple models. Chaos, Solitons and Fractals: X, 5, March 2020.
et al T. Yamana . Projection of covid-19 cases and deaths in the us as individual states re-open. wordpress.com Columbia University, May 2020.
